standards.label: Annotation properties need definitions
standards.guidance: Add a skos:definition for each annotation property so editors and downstream users can understand how the annotation should be used.

standards.label: Annotation properties need labels
standards.guidance: Add an rdfs:label for each annotation property so it has a readable display name in tools, exports, and documentation.

standards.label: Class definitions should be Aristotelian
standards.guidance: Rewrite class definitions so they identify the broader parent and the distinguishing differentia, rather than only giving loose or informal prose.

standards.label: Classes need definitions
standards.guidance: Add a skos:definition or accepted definition annotation for each class so curators and implementers know exactly what the class denotes.

standards.label: Classes should have examples
standards.guidance: Add skos:example content for classes where an example would clarify intended usage, especially for abstract or easily confused terms.

standards.label: Classes need curated-in-ontology
standards.guidance: Add cco:ont00001760 to each class so the ontology explicitly records where that class is curated and maintained.

standards.label: Classes need labels
standards.guidance: Add an rdfs:label for each class so people and software can identify the class without relying on the raw IRI.

standards.label: Classes need a BFO parent path
standards.guidance: Assert subclass relationships so each class has a justified parent chain to BFO:Entity or another accepted top-level parent in the modeled hierarchy.

standards.label: Classes need subclass assertions
standards.guidance: Add at least one rdfs:subClassOf assertion for each class so it is placed in the ontology hierarchy and can inherit intended semantics.

standards.label: Data properties need definitions
standards.guidance: Add a skos:definition for each data property so users understand the intended meaning of the literal value being asserted.

standards.label: Data properties need labels
standards.guidance: Add an rdfs:label for each data property so it has a readable name in tools, exports, and documentation.

standards.label: Datatype properties need definitions
standards.guidance: Add a skos:definition for each datatype property so users understand the intended meaning of the data value being asserted.

standards.label: Datatype properties need domains
standards.guidance: Add an rdfs:domain for each datatype property so consumers know which kinds of entities the property is intended to describe.

standards.label: Datatype properties need labels
standards.guidance: Add an rdfs:label for each datatype property so it has a readable name in tools, exports, and documentation.

standards.label: Datatype properties need ranges
standards.guidance: Add an rdfs:range for each datatype property so consumers know the expected literal datatype or value space.

standards.label: Resources need rdf:type assertions
standards.guidance: Add rdf:type assertions so each resource is explicitly declared as a class, property, individual, or other supported entity type.

standards.label: Named individuals need definitions
standards.guidance: Add a skos:definition for named individuals where the ontology treats them as curated knowledge artifacts that need clear meaning and usage notes.

standards.label: Named individuals need labels
standards.guidance: Add an rdfs:label for each named individual so it can be read and reviewed without relying on the IRI alone.

standards.label: Avoid mixed metatype punning
standards.guidance: Refactor entities so the same IRI is not reused across incompatible metatypes such as class, individual, and property unless there is a strong design reason.

standards.label: Object properties need definitions
standards.guidance: Add a skos:definition for each object property so its intended relational meaning is clear to curators and application developers.

standards.label: Object properties need domains
standards.guidance: Add an rdfs:domain for each object property so the ontology states what kinds of subjects the relation is intended to connect from.

standards.label: Object properties need labels
standards.guidance: Add an rdfs:label for each object property so users can understand and browse the relation without relying on the IRI alone.

standards.label: Object properties need ranges
standards.guidance: Add an rdfs:range for each object property so the ontology states what kinds of objects the relation is intended to connect to.

standards.label: Ontology should state access rights
standards.guidance: Add dcterms:accessRights on the ontology resource to communicate any restrictions or expectations about access and reuse.

standards.label: Ontology should use CC0 or CC BY 4.0
standards.guidance: Update the ontology license to an approved open license, ideally CC0 or CC BY 4.0, so reuse expectations are clear and aligned with project policy.

standards.label: Ontology needs a license
standards.guidance: Add dcterms:license on the ontology resource so users know the legal terms for reuse, distribution, and derivative work.

standards.label: Ontology should have versionInfo
standards.guidance: Add owl:versionInfo to the ontology so human-readable release information is available alongside the version IRI.

standards.label: Ontology needs a version IRI
standards.guidance: Add owl:versionIRI to the ontology resource so each release is uniquely identified and can be referenced unambiguously.

standards.label: versionInfo should use date or semver
standards.guidance: Revise owl:versionInfo so it includes either an ISO-style date or a semantic version pattern that is easy for humans to interpret.

standards.label: versionIRI and versionInfo must agree
standards.guidance: Align owl:versionIRI and owl:versionInfo so they refer to the same release identifier and do not communicate conflicting version values.

standards.label: Version IRI needs date or semver
standards.guidance: Revise owl:versionIRI so its release identifier includes either a date-based version or a semantic version string.

standards.label: Version IRI must be a valid IRI
standards.guidance: Correct owl:versionIRI so it is stored as an actual IRI and resolves to syntactically valid IRI content rather than a malformed or literal value.

standards.label: Version IRI should use ISO 8601 dates
standards.guidance: If your release strategy is date-based, format the date portion of owl:versionIRI using an ISO 8601 pattern so releases sort and compare cleanly.

standards.label: Labels should be unique
standards.guidance: Review duplicate rdfs:label values and disambiguate them where needed so labels remain reliable for search, editing, and downstream use.
